text: Handling deadlines is a crucial skill in both academic and professional life. Good time management helps you break large projects into smaller tasks, which makes them less overwhelming. Prioritization is equally important—focus on the most urgent or valuable steps first, rather than getting stuck on minor details. Sometimes, you may need to manage the scope of a task, meaning you adjust what you aim to deliver to fit the time available. Finally, open communication is key. If you foresee a delay, let your manager or team know early. This allows for adjustments and reduces stress, making deadlines more manageable for everyone.
